local_checks.raw_manifest_scan: Searched existing local Kubernetes, Kueue, and Volcano raw GitHub index and joined-comment corpora under personal-wiki/domains/ai_infra/raw/github. No network fetch was used. Selected issue numbers are visible in local index or joined-comment files with DRA, ResourceClaim, ResourceSlice, device-plugin, GPU, vGPU, Hami, scheduler, quota, preemption, panic, stuck, or failed scheduling terms.
local_checks.wiki_search: Searched Orchestration Scheduling Infrastructure, Kubernetes/Volcano/Kueue GitHub Closed Issues, AI Infra Coverage Map, coverage-map.json, loop-state.json, ingest.md, and prior continuation parent-1 through parent-4 manifests. Existing pages already promoted Kueue #1726/#696/#6143/#1407/#3094/#2867/#2941, Volcano #452/#2547/#3329/#2701/#3384/#3301/#2416/#2379, and SGLang #23627 as duplicate boundaries or prior issue-level signals; parent-5 selected only non-promoted Kubernetes DRA/device-plugin, Kueue DRA quota, and Volcano DRA/vGPU/device-plugin issue records.
local_checks.domain_index_scan: ai_infra wiki/index.md routes this work to Orchestration Scheduling Infrastructure, Kubernetes/Volcano/Kueue GitHub Closed Issues, and AI Infra Coverage Map; no new standalone page was needed.

duplicate_boundary_checks.result: Before parent-5, local scheduling issue signals covered Kueue admission/preemption/ResourceFlavor/MultiKueue examples, Volcano gang scheduling/preemption/vGPU/scheduler panic/upgrade examples, and SGLang Slurm cgroup cleanup. The selected Kubernetes DRA ResourceClaim/ResourceSlice, device-plugin, Kueue DRA quota borrowing, and Volcano DRA/vGPU scale issues were not already represented as durable text.
The corpus page documented counts, raw files, sync setup, and retrieval rules but did not yet summarize a DRA/device-plugin scheduling slice or list the selected parent-5 issue numbers.

The orchestration-scheduling layer was partial with candidate gaps for incidents, Slurm-on-Kubernetes bridge captures, scheduler benchmarks, and operator/device-plugin postmortems. Parent-5 adds only issue-level signals and preserves those remaining gaps.
Added a separate known_source entry for parent-5 DRA/device-plugin scheduling issue signals without mutating blocked_items or existing blocked coverage_gaps.

Parent-1 covered Corigine hardware, parent-2 SGLang scheduled crawler inference operations, parent-3 Yusur DPU hardware, and parent-4 NVIDIA H200 hardware. None promoted Kubernetes/Kueue/Volcano DRA or device-plugin scheduling issue signals.

gap_reason: The local GitHub issue corpora contained non-duplicate DRA, ResourceClaim/ResourceSlice, device-plugin, GPU/vGPU, quota, and scheduler-failure records that improve orchestration-scheduling coverage while staying within existing raw evidence and without claiming production postmortems or benchmarks.

promoted_issue_signals.signal: DRA scheduler failures around shared multi-node/per-node ResourceClaims, gang scheduling with shared claims, allocationMode All shared-counter panic, and CEL selector runtime errors on missing gpu.nvidia.com attributes.

DRA and legacy device-plugin coexistence signals in kubelet/device management: unhealthy-device endpoint drift, extended-resource cleanup leakage, multiple ResourceClaim prepare handling, and resourceClaimStatuses flapping.
DRA partitionable-device quota borrowing with OpenShift, Kueue Operator, NVIDIA GPU Operator, DRA Driver, and DRAPartitionableDevices context.
Volcano DRA ResourceClaimTemplate preparation failure, PreBind lock scheduling block, stale vGPU annotation over-subscription, Hami vGPU scale delay, and device-plugin reinstall OutOfSync boundaries.

duplicate_or_boundary_issues.reason: Previously promoted by task-3 as Kueue admission, ResourceQuota, preemption, ResourceFlavor, MultiKueue, extended-resource, or DRA concerns; not re-promoted.
Previously promoted by task-3 as Volcano gang scheduling, preemption, GPU sharing, vGPU, scheduler panic, or upgrade/quota examples; not re-promoted.
Previously noted as a downstream Slurm cgroup cleanup lead; parent-5 stays in Kubernetes/Kueue/Volcano local corpora.
Kept as a feature-boundary record for DRA extended resources and quota accounting, not as a runtime failure signal.
Same DRA quota-borrowing theme as Kueue #12207; treated as duplicate/parallel boundary rather than promoted alongside the Kueue issue.

non_promoted_boundaries: No new network source, crawler subscription, raw fetch, or external link probe was added; existing local raw issue corpora were sufficient.
No claim is made that the selected issue set is a complete comment corpus; Kubernetes and Kueue summaries still mark comment joins incomplete.
No production incident/postmortem, service-impact timeline, scheduler benchmark, Slurm-on-Kubernetes bridge deployment, or GPU-operator upgrade closure is claimed.
No parent-1 Corigine, parent-2 SGLang, parent-3 Yusur, parent-4 H200, or previously promoted task-3 Kueue/Volcano/SGLang issue signal was modified or re-promoted.
